pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh; confirmed in env.json description and task_utils.sh
Specified in task description; verified as the table name to be created
Queried actual Chinook database: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Customer returns 59 rows
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT City FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=1
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT Country FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=1
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT City FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=14
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT Country FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=14
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT City FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=46
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT Country FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=46
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT City FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=59
Chinook SQLite database: SELECT Country FROM Customer WHERE CustomerId=59
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Artist in Chinook SQLite database; also confirmed in env.json description
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Album in Chinook SQLite database; also confirmed in env.json description
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track in Chinook SQLite database; also confirmed in env.json description
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Invoice in Chinook SQLite database; also confirmed in env.json description

privileged_info_summary: The normalize_address_table task uses the real, publicly available Chinook database from the lerocha/chinook-database GitHub repository. The Customer table contains exactly 59 rows with addresses and contact information from real-world cities. The spot-check records are all accurate: Customer 1 is from São José dos Campos, Brazil; Customer 14 from Edmonton, Canada; Customer 46 from Dublin, Ireland; and Customer 59 from Bangalore, India. The database path is /home/ga/chinook.odb and the task expects to create a CustomerAddress table with 59 rows extracted from the Customer table.
